prompt:
The counter IDs in the sortid package finally bit us: two ingest workers restarted and started reissuing job-000001, and the blob store lists keys lexicographically so nothing comes back in creation order anyway. Ticket INGEST-412 says we grow the package with time-sortable IDs. The existing Generator stays exactly as is — half the fleet still uses it.

What I need:

- NewSortable(now func() time.Time) returning a Sortable whose Next() gives a 16-character lowercase-hex ID: the first 12 hex digits are the Unix millisecond timestamp from the injected clock, the last 4 hex digits are a per-millisecond sequence starting at 0. So IDs made later always sort later as plain strings, which is the whole point.

- Monotonicity no matter what the clock does. Two calls in the same millisecond bump the sequence. If the sequence would blow past ffff, roll into the next millisecond with sequence 0. And if the wall clock steps BACKWARDS (NTP does this to us weekly), never emit an ID that sorts before one we already handed out — keep using the last millisecond we stamped and keep counting.

- Parse(id string) returning the embedded timestamp (as a UTC time.Time, millisecond precision) and the sequence number, with an error for anything malformed: wrong length, characters outside 0-9a-f (uppercase included — canonical form is lowercase only).

- Sortable must be safe from multiple goroutines, same as Generator.

sortable_test.go is the acceptance contract; sortid_test.go pins the old counter behavior and must keep passing untouched. Don't modify either test file. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... has to go green.
